channel.title: Helm on Dijure | Digital Architectures with a hint of Java
channel.description: Recent content in Helm on Dijure | Digital Architectures with a hint of Java
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 by Dijure LLC · All rights reserved · Some design elements by Themefisher. Select images from Unsplash. Created using Hugo.

item.title: Operator SDK with Go
item.description:
In this lab, you will experience what it’s like to create and run a Kubernetes Operator that manages a custom resource. The Operator is created using the Operator SDK tool along with some Go code. This lab closely follows the Operator SDK documentation for building an Operator using Go .

The Operator SDK is a component of the Operator Framework, an open source tool kit to manage Kubernetes native applications, called Operators, in an effective, automated, and scalable way.

item.title: Storage with Redis
item.description:
Our microservices often need fast and relatively local data storage. Redis (REmote DIctionary Server) is an open source, in-memory, NoSQL key/value store that is used primarily as an application cache or quick-response database. It’s recognized for its speed, reliability, and features. It’s a popular choice for developers because it’s easy to hook to our applications for solving the data storage designs.

These next steps provide an ideal place to learn how to install and connect to a Redis application on Kubernetes.

item.title: Helm
item.description:
This lab teaches you how to use most of the features in Helm, a package manager for Kubernetes.

Helm is the best way to find, share, and use software built for Kubernetes.

In this lab, you will learn how to:

    ☐ Install and uninstall applications
    ☐ Inspect what a chart provides
    ☐ List public charts
    ☐ List and add more repositories
    ☐ Create a custom chart
    ☐ Update a chart

item.title: Helmfile
item.description:
This lab introduces you to the key features of Helmfile.

Helmfile builds on the concepts of Helm charts. If you are unfamiliar with Helm, try this lab first: Kubernetes Pipelines: Helm .

Helm is a package manager tool that bundles related Kubernetes YAML manifests and context settings for a set of related applications. The packages are grouped into Helm charts that are named, versioned, and stored in Helm registries. Charts along with injected context values are deployed to various Kubernetes targets.

item.title: RabbitMQ
item.description:
Robust messaging for applications to connect and scale. RabbitMQ is an open-source message broker software that implements the Advanced Message Queuing Protocol (AMQP).

These next steps provide an ideal place to start deploying and running one of your first application on Kubernetes. With a Kubernetes cluster and a CLI tool called kubectl and helm, a few steps will get RabbitMQ running.

In this lab, you will learn how to:
